sources.title: Attention Is All You Need
sources.notes: The foundational paper. Introduces the Transformer architecture with scaled dot-product attention, multi-head attention, sinusoidal positional encodings, encoder-decoder structure.

sources.title: The Annotated Transformer
sources.notes: Line-by-line PyTorch implementation of the original Transformer. Outstanding for understanding every component. Updated version uses modern PyTorch.

sources.title: The Illustrated Transformer
sources.notes: Gold-standard visual explainer. Beautiful diagrams showing attention flow, QKV projections, multi-head attention. Essential reference for visual design.

sources.title: The Transformer Family Version 2.0
sources.notes: Comprehensive survey-style blog covering attention variants, sparse attention, linearized attention, positional encodings, and efficiency improvements. Excellent taxonomic reference.

sources.title: RoFormer: Enhanced Transformer with Rotary Position Embedding
sources.notes: Introduces RoPE — the dominant positional encoding method in modern LLMs. Mathematically elegant rotation-based approach.

sources.title: Rotary Embeddings: A Relative Affair
sources.notes: Excellent intuitive and mathematical explanation of RoPE. Bridges the gap between the paper and implementation.

sources.title: On Layer Normalization in the Transformer Architecture
sources.notes: Rigorous analysis of Pre-LN vs Post-LN. Shows Pre-LN enables training without warmup. Gradient flow analysis.

sources.title: Efficient Transformers: A Survey
sources.notes: Comprehensive taxonomy of efficient attention mechanisms. Covers fixed/learnable patterns, memory, low-rank, kernel methods. Essential for modern variants chapter.

sources.title: FlashAttention: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Attention with IO-Awareness
sources.notes: IO-aware attention algorithm using tiling and recomputation. 2-4x wall-clock speedup. Foundational for modern training.

sources.title: FlashAttention-2: Faster Attention with Better Parallelism and Work Partitioning
sources.notes: Improved version with better GPU utilization. Further parallelism improvements.

sources.title: GLU Variants Improve Transformer
sources.notes: Introduces SwiGLU and GEGLU. Shows gated linear units improve Transformer FFN quality. Used in LLaMA, PaLM.

sources.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers
sources.notes: Encoder-only Transformer for bidirectional pre-training. Masked language modeling. Key architecture variant.

sources.title: Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners (GPT-2)
sources.notes: Decoder-only architecture. Demonstrates autoregressive pre-training at scale.

sources.title: Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with T5
sources.notes: Encoder-decoder architecture. Comprehensive study of pretraining objectives. Relative position biases.

sources.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
sources.notes: Power-law scaling relationships between model size, data, compute, and loss.

sources.title: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models (Chinchilla)
sources.notes: Chinchilla scaling laws. Shows most LLMs are undertrained relative to their size. Optimal data-to-params ratio.

sources.title: Switch Transformers: Scaling to Trillion Parameter Models
sources.notes: Simplifies MoE routing to top-1. Demonstrates scaling to trillion parameters with conditional computation.

sources.title: Longformer: The Long-Document Transformer
sources.notes: Local sliding window + global attention pattern. O(n) complexity. Practical long-document processing.

sources.title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale (ViT)
sources.notes: Vision Transformer. Splits images into patches, treats as token sequence. Shows Transformers can match CNNs at scale.

sources.title: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces
sources.notes: Selective state space model as alternative to attention. Linear-time inference. Competitive with Transformers on language.

sources.title: Root Mean Square Layer Normalization
sources.notes: Introduces RMSNorm. Simplifies LayerNorm by removing mean centering. Used in LLaMA, Gemma.

sources.title: Decoupled Weight Decay Regularization (AdamW)
sources.notes: Shows L2 regularization != weight decay in Adam. Introduces AdamW. Standard optimizer for Transformer training.

sources.title: The Illustrated Attention (Jay Alammar)
sources.notes: Precursor to Illustrated Transformer. Good for building up from seq2seq attention to self-attention.

sources.title: The Illustrated GPT-2 (Jay Alammar)
sources.notes: Excellent visual walkthrough of decoder-only architecture and autoregressive generation.

sources.title: Let's build GPT: from scratch, in code, spelled out
sources.notes: 2-hour masterclass building a GPT from scratch in PyTorch. Covers every component with clear explanations. Essential implementation reference.

sources.title: Attention in transformers, visually explained (3Blue1Brown)
sources.notes: Beautiful geometric visualizations of attention. QKV as geometric operations. Exceptional for building intuition.

sources.title: But what is a GPT? Visual intro to Transformers (3Blue1Brown)
sources.notes: Part 1 of the Transformer series. Embedding space visualization. Excellent visual foundation.

sources.title: Attention Mechanism — Umar Jamil
sources.notes: Detailed code-along for attention mechanism with clear mathematical exposition. Good for implementation understanding.

sources.title: Transformer from Scratch — Umar Jamil
sources.notes: Full Transformer implementation in PyTorch with excellent whiteboard explanations. Covers all major components.

sources.title: Positional Encoding Explained — Umar Jamil
sources.notes: Deep dive into sinusoidal encodings with math derivations. Also covers learned embeddings.

sources.title: StatQuest: Transformer Neural Networks Clearly Explained
sources.notes: Clear step-by-step explanation suitable for solidifying fundamentals. Good for review.

sources.title: Llama explained — Umar Jamil
sources.notes: Covers RMSNorm, RoPE, SwiGLU, and GQA as used in LLaMA. Good modern architecture reference.

sources.title: Formal Algorithms for Transformers
sources.notes: Pseudocode for all Transformer components. Good for precise algorithmic understanding.

sources.title: Understanding and Coding the Self-Attention Mechanism (Sebastian Raschka)
sources.notes: Step-by-step Python/PyTorch implementation with excellent commentary. Modern and well-maintained.

sources.title: The Random Transformer (LessWrong / Neel Nanda)
sources.notes: Mechanistic interpretability perspective on Transformer internals. Residual stream view.

sources.title: Transformer Inference Arithmetic (Kipply)
sources.notes: Detailed FLOP counting, memory bandwidth analysis, KV cache sizing. Essential for the engineering layer.

sources.title: Gaussian Error Linear Units (GELUs)
sources.notes: Original GELU paper. Probabilistic interpretation of activation gating.

sources.title: Train Short, Test Long: Attention with Linear Biases (ALiBi)
sources.notes: No position embeddings. Linear bias on attention scores. Strong length generalization.

sources.title: A Survey of Large Language Models
sources.notes: Comprehensive LLM survey covering architecture decisions, training recipes, and scaling.

sources.title: Transformer Math 101 (EleutherAI)
sources.notes: Parameter counting, FLOP estimation, memory requirements. Practical engineering reference.

sources.title: Performer: Rethinking Attention with Performers (FAVOR+)
sources.notes: Linearized attention via random feature maps. Theoretical elegance but limited practical adoption.

sources.title: BigBird: Transformers for Longer Sequences
sources.notes: Random + window + global attention pattern. Theoretical Turing-completeness proof for sparse attention.

sources.title: How GPT3 Works — Visualizations and Animations (Jay Alammar)
sources.notes: High-level visual overview of autoregressive generation and in-context learning.

sources.title: The Transformer Blueprint: A Holistic Guide (Bloem)
sources.notes: Excellent holistic explanation with clear mathematics and PyTorch code. Good intermediate reference.

sources.title: Mixture of Experts Explained (HuggingFace)
sources.notes: Practical guide to MoE covering routing, load balancing, training, and inference. Good diagrams.

sources.title: FlashAttention Explained (Aleksa Gordic)
sources.notes: Accessible explanation of FlashAttention with tiling diagrams and memory hierarchy.

sources.title: Layer Normalization (Ba et al., 2016)
sources.notes: Original LayerNorm paper. Foundation for understanding normalization in Transformers.

sources.title: minGPT (Karpathy)
sources.notes: Minimal GPT implementation in PyTorch. Clean, readable code. Good for labs.

sources.title: nanoGPT (Karpathy)
sources.notes: Most practical GPT training codebase. Covers mixed precision, gradient accumulation, FlashAttention. Essential lab reference.

sources.title: Attention? Attention! (Lilian Weng, 2018)
sources.notes: Historical progression from Bahdanau attention to self-attention. Good for understanding motivation.

sources.title: d2l.ai — Transformer Chapter
sources.notes: Interactive textbook with runnable code. Good pedagogical sequencing.

sources.title: r/MachineLearning — Best resources for understanding Transformers
sources.notes: Community-curated resource lists. Good for discovering lesser-known explainers.

sources.title: StackOverflow — Transformer implementation questions
sources.notes: Practical implementation Q&A. Good for common pitfalls and bugs.

sources.title: HuggingFace Transformers documentation
sources.notes: Reference implementation docs. Architecture configs for BERT, GPT-2, T5, LLaMA.

sources.title: PyTorch nn.Transformer documentation
sources.notes: Official PyTorch Transformer API. Useful reference but not ideal for learning.

sources.title: Yannic Kilcher — Attention Is All You Need paper review
sources.notes: Paper walkthrough with research context. Good supplementary viewing.

sources.title: Xavier Initialization and Why It Matters for Transformers
sources.notes: Weight initialization context for understanding training stability.

sources.title: Jamba: A Hybrid Transformer-Mamba Language Model
sources.notes: Practical hybrid architecture combining attention and Mamba layers with MoE.

sources.title: DeiT: Training data-efficient image transformers
sources.notes: Data-efficient ViT training with distillation. Important for understanding ViT practical deployment.

sources.title: GQA: Training Generalized Multi-Query Attention
sources.notes: Grouped-query attention as interpolation between MHA and MQA. Used in LLaMA 2+.

sources.title: How to Train Really Large Models on Many GPUs (Lilian Weng)
sources.notes: Distributed training, model parallelism, data parallelism, ZeRO. Good context for scaling discussion.

sources.title: Transformer Feed-Forward Layers Are Key-Value Memories (Geva et al.)
sources.notes: Shows FFN layers store factual knowledge as key-value memories. Foundational for understanding FFN role.
